spec_match.rationale: Hits eight distinct component archetypes (buttons primary/outline/ghost, badges filled/outline/soft, tab strip, toggles, progress, focus rings, header bar, links) — exercises AccentColor across nearly every <color> context the spec accepts.

interactive.rationale: Eight preset swatches plus color picker drive a real component gallery; tab strip is clickable, toggles flip, focus rings actually appear on focus. Past the static-card bar by a wide margin.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises a gallery of components themed with AccentColor that update on accent change; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Solid component coverage but: the AccentColorText algorithm uses a manual ratio comparison rather than the spec's exact contrast computation; no PWA-context probe; soft-badge uses color-mix with white instead of the actual neutral-paper variable so it can fail on dark themes; no forced-colors story.

references.rationale: see-also lists three sibling concepts but does NOT include the chromestatus.com/feature/5068127364186112 link directly — only the parent feature page does. Per CLAUDE.md rule 3, every page must include the chromestatus link.

design_system.rationale: Mostly variable-based, but :root sets --sim-accent to color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-blue) 58%, var(--text-black)) which is a defensible authored token, AND the soft-badge color-mix uses literal 'white' rather than --bg-paper which breaks dark-mode hygiene if the design system gains a dark mode.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus link directly to see-also
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md mandates every page link the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> URL for self-heal. This page only links sibling concepts. Add the chromestatus reference + the CSS Color 4 spec link as the parent index does.

openQuestions.title: Replace literal 'white' in color-mix with --bg-paper
openQuestions.detail: .badge-soft uses color-mix(in srgb, var(--sim-accent) 15%, white). Replace 'white' with var(--bg-paper) so the soft badge tone follows the design system's paper colour. Same applies to any literal hex elsewhere — none should remain.

openQuestions.title: Wire the accent-color property
openQuestions.detail: The page styles a <progress> with ::-webkit-progress-value but doesn't demonstrate the simpler accent-color: AccentColor; rule that natively themes form controls (checkbox, radio, range, progress) in one line. Add a row showing native form controls with accent-color set to AccentColor — that's a major use case the feature unlocks.

openQuestions.title: Show forced-colors / installed-PWA fallback
openQuestions.detail: Same pattern as the other concepts in this feature: probe matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)') and forced-colors:active, and show what each component looks like in fallback mode.

summary: Most comprehensive of the AccentColor concepts in terms of component variety, but it ships without the chromestatus reference link and uses a couple of literal colour values that break the design system. The accent-color property is also a missed angle for form-control theming.
